meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI to Solve Project Scaling
content.seo_description: Master advanced debugging with AI to overcome scaling bottlenecks. This case study teaches students to use Antigravity and Claude Code for professional apps.
content.answer_block: To scale your projects, shift from manual debugging to AI-assisted root cause analysis. By integrating Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for iterative CLI-based refactoring, you transform complex bottlenecks into modular, testable components. This workflow allows students to build enterprise-grade software faster while mastering modern AI-augmented development patterns.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a codebase that has become a tangled web of technical debt. Your initial MVP worked perfectly, but as you added features, the architecture began to buckle under its own weight. The thrill of coding has been replaced by the paralyzing frustration of 'can't scale projects,' leaving you stuck in a loop of fixing one bug only to break two others.

This isn't a failure of your ability; it's a rite of passage. Scaling requires a shift from writing code to engineering systems. By leveraging the combined power of Antigravity's structural insights and Claude Code's autonomous execution, you can bypass the manual slog. It's time to stop fighting your code and start orchestrating it.

steps.title: Mapping Architectural Bottlenecks
steps.description: Visualize your dependency graph to identify where circular dependencies or bloated modules are choking your performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project directory structure and generate a visual dependency map identifying high-coupling modules that hinder vertical scaling.

steps.title: Isolation of Scalability Killers
steps.description: Isolate the functions causing execution latency using AI-driven static analysis to pinpoint hidden inefficiencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the identified bottleneck modules and suggest a refactoring strategy to decouple business logic from external service calls.

steps.title: Automated Refactoring Cycles
steps.description: Execute incremental refactors to modularize code without manual overhead, ensuring unit tests remain passing throughout.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a refactoring plan to implement a dependency injection pattern for the core service layer to improve testability and scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'refactor dependency injection in service layer' --verify

steps.title: Implementing Robust Error Boundaries
steps.description: Inject AI-generated error handling middleware to ensure your application fails gracefully under high load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest optimized error-handling middleware patterns that prevent cascading failures in our current service architecture.

steps.title: Validation and Performance Benchmarking
steps.description: Use Claude Code to run automated stress tests against your newly refactored modules to confirm performance gains.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a performance regression test script that targets the previously identified bottlenecks to validate the refactor's success.

pro_tips: Always use the 'context-window-first' approach: feed your architectural map into Claude Code before asking for specific refactors.
Treat your AI as a Senior Engineer: ask it to explain the 'why' behind a refactor to build your own intuition for system design.
Leverage Antigravity to identify 'God Objects'—classes that do too much—and split them before they become impossible to refactor.
Use CLI-based AI tools to keep your development flow in the terminal, preventing context switching between IDE and browser.
Implement automated documentation generation via your AI agent to ensure your newly scaled architecture remains maintainable for your future self.
content.next_steps: Once you have refactored your modules, deploy your project to a staging environment and run a load test to observe how the system handles concurrent requests. Document your findings to add a 'Scalability Optimization' section to your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to break through the scaling ceiling that stops most developers. Don't let your code become a legacy burden—take control of your architecture today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
